scenario_title: Duplicate add_rr for the same RR in one UPDATE
test_rationale: This test isolates an underspecified corner of RFC 2136 update processing: the UPDATE section contains two syntactically identical add_rr operations for the same owner, type, class, TTL, and RDATA. Some servers may collapse the duplicate and commit the change once, returning NOERROR; others may treat the repeated addition as an error and reject the transaction. Querying the touched name after the UPDATE makes the resulting RRset state directly observable and provides a binary oracle across BIND and Knot.

group_1.behavior: Accepts the UPDATE with NOERROR and creates a single A RRset member for host2.campus.edu. despite the duplicate add_rr entries.
group_1.post_state: host2.campus.edu. has one A RR in the rrset.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the UPDATE as an error when the same add_rr appears twice, or otherwise handles the duplicate differently so the transaction does not commit.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4 defines add semantics for an individual RR but does not specify how a server must process two identical add_rr entries within the same UPDATE message. RFC 2181 §5.2 defines RRset equality and identity at the data model level, but not update-time duplicate elision or error handling. As a result, implementations can plausibly diverge in both the UPDATE reply and the resulting zone state.
